title: Turn Covermint from spike into a configurable desktop widget
description:
## Goal
Track the next round of work needed to make Covermint feel portable, configurable, and polished instead of a one-off spike.

## Requested follow-ups
- keep the project branding media-player-agnostic
- remove system-specific dependencies and oddities
- support custom placement
- add an extensible transition system (starting with fade / flip)
- support a border with transparency
- improve custom sizing controls
- ship a stronger README

## Success criteria
- The work is split into concrete, independently shippable tickets.
- The project has a documented backlog in Beads.
close_reason: Completed the tracked roadmap slice: Covermint is now configurable, branded, documented, cached, and polished enough to graduate from the original spike.

title: Remove system-specific assumptions and odd dependencies
description:
## Problem
The current spike is tailored to the original environment and needs a portability pass.

## Scope
- audit compositor / runtime assumptions
- document the minimal required system dependencies
- remove hard-coded behavior that only makes sense on one setup
- keep the runtime story simple for Linux / Wayland users

## Done when
- setup instructions are clearer
- avoidable machine-specific quirks are removed or isolated
close_reason: Made player selection default to auto, removed the GTK 4.10-only monitor description dependency, lowered the GTK API floor to 4.8, and updated docs

title: Refresh placement when monitor state changes
description:
## Goal
Keep artwork placement aligned when monitor selection or monitor geometry changes after startup.

## Scope
- refresh selected monitor during the existing poll loop
- recompute placement from current geometry
- avoid noisy logging if nothing changed

## Done when
Moving between monitor states no longer requires restarting Covermint to re-anchor the artwork.
close_reason: Recompute monitor selection and placement during polling so geometry or monitor-state changes no longer require restart

title: Cache artwork locally
description:
## Goal
Avoid redownloading the same artwork URL repeatedly and make artwork swaps feel more reliable.

## Scope
- store artwork by URL-derived cache key
- reuse cached files before hitting the network
- keep the cache path simple and user-local

## Done when
Repeated tracks or temporary network issues do not force unnecessary redownloads.
close_reason: Added a simple user-local artwork cache keyed by URL so repeated artwork loads can reuse local files before hitting the network

title: Add example user service
description:
## Goal
Make it easy to run Covermint as a persistent user service.

## Scope
- add an example systemd user unit
- document how to install and customize it
- keep the unit simple and Linux-user friendly

## Done when
A user can copy the unit, adjust flags, and keep Covermint running automatically.
close_reason: Added an example systemd user unit and documented how to install and customize it

title: Trim artwork cache
description:
## Goal
Keep the local artwork cache from growing forever.

## Scope
- apply a simple retention policy
- keep the implementation lightweight
- avoid slowing down normal artwork refreshes too much

## Done when
The cache remains useful without becoming an unbounded pile of files.
close_reason: Added lightweight cache trimming by age and file count so the local artwork cache stays bounded without extra configuration

title: Polish example systemd unit
description:
## Goal
Remove placeholder details and make the example user service feel copy-paste ready.

## Scope
- remove fake metadata
- keep the service example minimal
- align the README with the cleaned-up unit

## Done when
The example unit looks intentional and does not contain obviously fake placeholders.
close_reason: Removed the placeholder documentation URL and made the example unit explicitly customizable without fake metadata

title: Support file:// artwork URLs
description:
## Goal
Load artwork from local file URLs exposed by MPRIS, not just remote HTTP(S) artwork.

## Done when
- file:// artwork URLs render successfully
- remote URLs keep working
- docs mention the capability where relevant
close_reason: Covermint now loads file:// artwork URLs directly while keeping remote HTTP(S) artwork cached.

title: Add internal/external monitor aliases
description:
## Goal
Make monitor selection more portable by supporting semantic aliases instead of raw connector names.

## Done when
- --monitor internal selects the built-in display when available
- --monitor external selects a non-internal display when available
- docs mention the aliases
close_reason: Added internal/external monitor aliases, labeled monitor listings, and documented the aliases.

title: Optionally keep artwork visible while paused
description:
## Goal
Let Covermint keep the last artwork visible when playback is paused instead of always hiding it.

## Done when
- a CLI flag enables paused artwork visibility
- default behavior stays the same
- docs mention the flag
close_reason: Added --show-paused so Covermint can keep artwork visible while playback is paused without changing the default behavior.

title: Add configurable corner radius
description:
## Goal
Allow Covermint to round the artwork frame corners for a softer, more polished look.

## Done when
- a CLI flag controls corner radius
- radius integrates with existing border styling
- docs mention the option
close_reason: Added --corner-radius so the artwork frame can use rounded corners alongside the existing border styling.

title: Add player discovery command
description:
## Goal
Help users discover available MPRIS player names without guessing.

## Done when
- a CLI flag lists available players and exits
- the README documents it
- existing player selection keeps working
close_reason: Added --list-players to print available MPRIS player names and documented it.

title: Add custom placement controls
description:
## Goal
Let users choose where the artwork appears instead of hard-coding bottom-right placement.

## Scope
- support choosing edges / corners / offsets
- keep the CLI easy to understand
- make placement work across monitors
close_reason: Added placement presets plus per-axis offsets and documented the new CLI flags

title: Allow disabling remote artwork cache
description:
## Goal
Let users opt out of Covermint's remote artwork cache when they prefer always-fresh fetches or stateless behavior.

## Done when
- a CLI flag disables cache reads and writes
- default behavior remains cached
- docs mention the flag
close_reason: Added --no-cache to bypass remote artwork cache reads and writes while keeping cached behavior as the default.

title: Add overall artwork opacity control
description:
## Goal
Let users blend Covermint more gently into the desktop with a simple overall opacity control.

## Done when
- a CLI flag sets overall artwork opacity
- values are validated sensibly
- docs mention the option
close_reason: Added --opacity with 0.0-1.0 validation so Covermint can blend more softly into the desktop.

title: Allow monitor selection by index
description:
## Goal
Let users select monitors by the numeric index shown in --list-monitors output.

## Done when
- --monitor accepts 0 / 1 / #0 style selectors
- docs mention the behavior
- existing alias/name matching keeps working
close_reason: Added monitor index selectors like 0 and #0, matching the indices shown by --list-monitors.

title: Keep artwork window size stable across images
description:
## Goal
Prevent Covermint from resizing/repositioning when artwork dimensions or transition frames vary.

## Done when
- the layer-shell window keeps a stable configured size
- placement math uses the outer window size
- smaller artwork still scales up into the configured frame
close_reason: Fixed the artwork surface to the configured frame size, kept transition resizing inside that fixed stage, and updated placement math to use the outer window size including borders.

title: Constrain artwork to fixed frame bounds
description:
## Goal
Ensure GTK always lays artwork out inside the configured frame size instead of letting natural image size overflow the surface.

## Done when
- large artwork scales down to fit the frame
- small artwork can scale up to fit the frame
- monitor placement stays stable because picture natural size no longer leaks into layout
close_reason: Pictures now shrink and expand inside a fixed stage, so large artwork no longer overflows and small artwork scales up into the configured frame.

title: Polish rounded-corner rendering
description:
## Goal
Make rounded corners feel production-ready across static and animated states.

## Context
`--corner-radius` already exists. This follow-up tracks polish work rather than the initial feature.

## Scope
- verify clipping stays clean during fade/flip transitions
- ensure borders, opacity, and transparent backgrounds still render cleanly at larger radii
- decide whether a few preset/default radius values should be recommended in docs

## Done when
Rounded corners look intentional across common artwork sizes and transition modes.
close_reason: Added an inner artwork clipping stage so rounded corners stay cleaner during static and animated states, and documented a practical radius range.

title: Add configurable cache size limits and LRU eviction
description:
## Goal
Replace the current lightweight cache retention policy with a more predictable bounded cache.

## Context
Caching exists today, but eviction is still a simple retention policy rather than a configurable size-aware system.

## Scope
- add explicit cache limits, such as total size and/or item count
- prefer an access-aware policy like LRU so frequently reused artwork stays hot
- keep defaults simple for users who do not want to tune cache behavior
- document the tradeoffs for `--no-cache` versus bounded caching

## Done when
Cache growth is bounded by explicit limits and repeated artwork is preferentially retained.
close_reason: Added configurable cache entry-count and size limits, plus simple LRU-style reuse by refreshing cached entry mtimes on hits.

title: Explore higher-fidelity 3D / GL transitions
description:
## Goal
Push the transition system beyond GTK-friendly fade/flip into richer 3D-feeling motion.

## Context
Current `flip` is intentionally not a true 3D transform.

## Scope
- prototype at least one higher-fidelity 3D transition
- evaluate whether GTK-only rendering is enough or whether a GL-backed path is justified
- define reduced-motion and compatibility fallbacks
- keep runtime overhead and startup impact reasonable

## Done when
There is a clear implementation path for a 3D/GL transition mode, with graceful fallback behavior.
close_reason: Added a new hinge pseudo-3D transition and documented the current GTK-first path, GL tradeoffs, and reduced-motion fallbacks.

title: Create logo asset for startup splash and branding
description:
## Goal
Create a simple Covermint logo or wordmark that can anchor the startup splash and other branded surfaces.

## Scope
- choose a direction that works on transparent and dark backgrounds
- produce lightweight assets suitable for startup rendering
- keep the style compatible with the current Covermint name and desktop-widget positioning

## Done when
A reusable logo asset exists in the repo for the splash screen and docs.
close_reason: Added the grungy Covermint logo asset under assets/branding for splash and branding use.

title: Add startup splash screen
description:
## Goal
Add a startup splash that makes launch feel intentional before player state and artwork are ready.

## Context
This depends on having a logo/brand asset available.

## Scope
- decide when the splash appears, how long it can remain visible, and when it should disappear
- choose whether it shares the layer-shell surface or uses a separate startup window
- incorporate the finalized logo asset
- avoid distracting flashes when startup is already fast

## Done when
Covermint has a polished startup experience with a clear exit path into the normal artwork view.
close_reason: Added an embedded startup splash using the Covermint logo, with a delayed reveal to avoid fast-start flashes and a clean handoff into the normal artwork view.

title: Add an extensible transition system
description:
## Goal
Support artwork transitions and make the system easy to extend.

## Start with
- fade
- flip (if practical with GTK)
- a small abstraction that can grow into more transition types later

## Notes
Keep performance and compositor compatibility in mind.
close_reason: Added an extensible transition hook with fade support, transition duration control, and spun off flip as follow-up sp-czm.8

title: Improve automatic player selection
description:
## Goal
When --player auto is used, pick a sensible active media player instead of relying on playerctl's default target.

## Done when
- active players like Spotify are preferred over stopped browser instances
- automatic selection still works when only one player is present
- no explicit --player flag is required for common setups
close_reason: Automatic player selection now prefers playing players and then players with artwork, so stopped browser MPRIS entries no longer hide active players like Spotify.

title: Keep startup splash above first artwork briefly
description:
## Goal
Show the startup splash long enough to read even when artwork is already cached or loads immediately.

## Done when
- the splash stays visible briefly on startup
- cover art can load underneath it immediately
- the window still hides after the splash if no artwork is available
close_reason: Startup splash now stays above the first resolved state for a short minimum duration, then fades out while preserving the underlying artwork or hiding the window if nothing loaded.

title: Scale startup splash logo within the artwork panel
description:
## Goal
Keep the startup splash logo visually smaller than the cover-art frame so it reads like an overlay, not a full replacement.

## Done when
- the splash logo is centered
- it uses about two thirds of the artwork panel
- underlying artwork can still load beneath it
close_reason: Startup splash logo is now centered and constrained to roughly two thirds of the artwork frame instead of filling the entire panel.

title: Support borders with transparency
description:
## Goal
Allow the artwork frame to have a visible border, including alpha / translucent styling.

## Scope
- configurable border width
- configurable border color / opacity
- keep the default subtle and wallpaper-friendly
close_reason: Added configurable border width/color via GTK CSS, including alpha-friendly css color values, and documented the new flags

title: Improve custom sizing controls
description:
## Goal
Make size configuration more flexible than the current single square size flag.

## Scope ideas
- clearer size flags
- optional width / height control
- better documentation for sizing behavior

## Done when
Users can intentionally choose the widget size without guessing.
close_reason: Added width and height flags while keeping --size as a shorthand, and updated placement math and docs

title: Write a polished README
description:
## Goal
Replace the spike-style README with project-quality documentation.

## Scope
- explain what Covermint is
- list requirements and limitations
- show common commands / examples
- document the Beads workflow and roadmap
- make planned features visible without overselling current functionality
close_reason: README rewritten with Beads workflow, requirements, examples, and roadmap

title: Rename project to Covermint
description:
## Goal
Rename the project so it is not tied to Spotify.

## Scope
- rename the crate / binary to Covermint
- update user-facing strings and docs
- update Beads backlog wording
- rename the working folder if practical at this stage

## Done when
The old name no longer appears in normal project surfaces.
close_reason: Repository surfaces now use Covermint consistently; the local checkout folder rename is intentionally left to the user outside the running session.

comments.author: dev
comments.text: Reopened: Crate/app/docs rename is done, but the working folder could not be renamed because /home/tom/code is root-owned and denied the move.

title: Add flip transition mode
description:
## Goal
Extend the new transition system with a flip-style artwork change animation.

## Context
Fade support exists now and the transition hook is in place. Flip needs a GTK-friendly implementation that stays smooth and does not feel distracting.

## Done when
- a flip mode is available from the CLI
- the motion feels intentional, not gimmicky
- fallback / reduced-motion behavior remains simple
close_reason: Added flip as a GTK-friendly squeeze/swap transition mode and documented the new CLI option

title: Polish transition easing
description:
## Goal
Make transitions feel more professional by adding easing instead of purely linear motion.

## Scope
- use eased timing for fade
- give flip a subtle springy feel without looking gimmicky
- keep motion restrained and desktop-friendly

## Done when
Transitions look deliberate, smooth, and a little playful without being exaggerated.
close_reason: Added eased fade timing and a restrained springy expansion curve for flip so transitions feel smoother and more polished
